perturbation.original_text: Each party hereto agrees not to use the Confidential Information received by such party as a Receiving Party other than for the Purpose of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereto agrees not to use the Information received by such party as a Receiving Party other than for the Purpose of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'Confidential' from 'Confidential Information' in Section 2, the agreement now vaguely restricts the use of *any* information received, rather than just confidential information, which is what the agreement is supposed to govern. This broadens the scope of the non-use clause beyond the stated purpose of the agreement. Now this section contradicts section 1's definition of Confidential information, making enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” as used in this Agreement means all information regarding a Disclosing Party’s business disclosed or made available to a Receiving Party by a Disclosing Party, or otherwise acquired by Rece iving Party, in connection with the Purpose of this Agreement, including, without limitation, any information relating to a Disclosing Party’s business, products, technology, data, engineering data or drawings, software (including all algorithms, methods, techniques and processes revealed by such software), customers, customer lists, customer needs and requirements, documentation, training materials, vendors, intellectual property, marketing, financial, projections, trade secrets, proprietary information or other information, whether in electronic, oral or written form, and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party which contain or reflect such information. The existence of the Purpose of this Agreement, this Agreement and the terms contained herein shall be de emed to be Confidential Information under this Agreement. Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession free from any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party, (c) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non- confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not prohibited from transmitting such info rmation by a contractual, legal, or other obligation, or (d) it was developed by employees or agents of the Receiving Party independently of and without reference to any information communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The change removes the word 'Confidential' from 'Confidential Information', broadening the scope of the non-use clause to *any* information received, rather than just confidential information as originally intended and defined in the 'Confidential Information' section. This contradicts the detailed definition of 'Confidential Information' elsewhere in the document, which specifies the types of information considered confidential and provides exceptions. By removing 'Confidential', the changed text now restricts the use of all information regardless of confidentiality, creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and no right or license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: All Information is and will remain the exclusive property of Disclosing Party, and no right or license is granted to Receiving Party with respect to any Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Confidential' before 'Information' in Section 3, concerning ownership, the clause now asserts that *all* information disclosed is the exclusive property of the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts the carefully defined scope of 'Confidential Information' in Section 1 and the exceptions carved out there. It creates a scenario where general information, not intended to be confidential, could be claimed as proprietary, making enforcement of the NDA terms uncertain. This contradicts Section 1, making enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” as used in this Agreement means all information regarding a Disclosing Party’s business disclosed or made available to a Receiving Party by a Disclosing Party, or otherwise acquired by Rece iving Party, in connection with the Purpose of this Agreement, including, without limitation, any information relating to a Disclosing Party’s business, products, technology, data, engineering data or drawings, software (including all algorithms, methods, techniques and processes revealed by such software), customers, customer lists, customer needs and requirements, documentation, training materials, vendors, intellectual property, marketing, financial, projections, trade secrets, proprietary information or other information, whether in electronic, oral or written form, and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party which contain or reflect such information. The existence of the Purpose of this Agreement, this Agreement and the terms contained herein shall be de emed to be Confidential Information under this Agreement. Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession free from any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party, (c) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non- confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not prohibited from transmitting such info rmation by a contractual, legal, or other obligation, or (d) it was developed by employees or agents of the Receiving Party independently of and without reference to any information communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the scope of property rights to 'Confidential Information,' as defined elsewhere. The changed text broadens this to 'Information,' encompassing all disclosed information, including items specifically excluded in the 'Confidential Information' definition in the contradicted text. This creates a direct conflict and a strong contradiction because the Receiving Party could be restricted from using information they are explicitly allowed to use under the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall govern all communications of Confidential Information between the parties until terminated by either party; provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure with respect to Confidential Information shall continue beyond termination until: (a) such time as the Confidential Information is no {J1872881.2} 3 longer deemed to be Confidential Information as more fully set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement or (b) upon the written consent of the Disclosing Party that the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure have ended.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall govern all communications of Information between the parties until terminated by either party; provided, however, the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure with respect to Information shall continue beyond termination until: (a) such time as the Information is no {J1872881.2} 3 longer deemed to be Confidential Information as more fully set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement or (b) upon the written consent of the Disclosing Party that the obligations of confidentiality and non-disclosure have ended.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Confidential' from the phrase 'Confidential Information' at the start of Section 9, but maintaining it later in the same sentence and referencing Section 1 (the Confidential Information definition), creates a critical contradiction. Now, the initial clause seems to govern *all* exchanged information, a very broad scope, while the exceptions still refer to 'Confidential Information' as specifically defined. This renders the section ambiguous: does it apply to all information, or just the narrower defined confidential information? This is further contradicted by referencing Section 1 which defines Confidential Information, making enforcement impossible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” as used in this Agreement means all information regarding a Disclosing Party’s business disclosed or made available to a Receiving Party by a Disclosing Party, or otherwise acquired by Rece iving Party, in connection with the Purpose of this Agreement, including, without limitation, any information relating to a Disclosing Party’s business, products, technology, data, engineering data or drawings, software (including all algorithms, methods, techniques and processes revealed by such software), customers, customer lists, customer needs and requirements, documentation, training materials, vendors, intellectual property, marketing, financial, projections, trade secrets, proprietary information or other information, whether in electronic, oral or written form, and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party which contain or reflect such information. The existence of the Purpose of this Agreement, this Agreement and the terms contained herein shall be de emed to be Confidential Information under this Agreement. Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession free from any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party, (c) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non- confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not prohibited from transmitting such info rmation by a contractual, legal, or other obligation, or (d) it was developed by employees or agents of the Receiving Party independently of and without reference to any information communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' in Section 9, aligning with the definition in Section 1. The changed text removes 'Confidential' from 'Confidential Information' at the beginning of Section 9, but keeps it later in the sentence and maintains the reference to Section 1. This creates a contradiction because the start of Section 9 now refers to 'Information' generally, while the rest of the section, and Section 1, define 'Confidential Information', making it unclear whether Section 9 applies to all information or just the defined 'Confidential Information.' This alteration directly conflicts with the definition in Section 1, as section 9 is now governing a much broader scope of information than intended, leading to unenforceability and qualifies as Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Both parties agree not to issue or release any articles, advertising, publicity or other matter relating to the existence of this Agreement or any Confidential Information (including the fact that a meeting or discussion has taken place between the parties) or mentioning or implying the name of the other party, except as may be required by law and then only after providing the other party with an opportunity to review and comment thereon.
perturbation.changed_text: Both parties agree not to issue or release any articles, advertising, publicity or other matter relating to the existence of this Agreement or any Information (including the fact that a meeting or discussion has taken place between the parties) or mentioning or implying the name of the other party, except as may be required by law and then only after providing the other party with an opportunity to review and comment thereon.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 10, by changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Information', the prohibition on publicity now extends to *any* information related to the agreement. This drastically broadens the scope, prohibiting parties from discussing anything related to the agreement, regardless of confidentiality. This contradicts the core principle of the agreement, which is to protect *confidential* information, not all information. The parties could reasonably assume that general details (e.g., 'we discussed project X') would be permissible, but this change forbids it. In this scenario it contradicts Section 1, making enforcement difficult.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. “Confidential Information” as used in this Agreement means all information regarding a Disclosing Party’s business disclosed or made available to a Receiving Party by a Disclosing Party, or otherwise acquired by Rece iving Party, in connection with the Purpose of this Agreement, including, without limitation, any information relating to a Disclosing Party’s business, products, technology, data, engineering data or drawings, software (including all algorithms, methods, techniques and processes revealed by such software), customers, customer lists, customer needs and requirements, documentation, training materials, vendors, intellectual property, marketing, financial, projections, trade secrets, proprietary information or other information, whether in electronic, oral or written form, and all notes, analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by the Receiving Party which contain or reflect such information. The existence of the Purpose of this Agreement, this Agreement and the terms contained herein shall be de emed to be Confidential Information under this Agreement. Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party, (b) was in the Receiving Party’s possession free from any obligation of confidence at the time it was communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party, (c) becomes available to the Receiving Party on a non- confidential basis from a source other than the Disclosing Party, provided that such source is not prohibited from transmitting such info rmation by a contractual, legal, or other obligation, or (d) it was developed by employees or agents of the Receiving Party independently of and without reference to any information communicated to the Receiving Party by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts publicity regarding 'Confidential Information'. The changed text broadens this restriction to *any* 'Information'. This contradicts the contradicted text (Section 1) which defines 'Confidential Information' with specific exclusions (e.g., public information, pre-existing knowledge). By prohibiting publicity of all information, the changed text negates the exclusions defined in Section 1, as even non-confidential information cannot be discussed. The original text allows discussion of information that is not confidential according to Section 1, but the changed text does not, creating a strong contradiction.
